problem_addressed: Label deficiency and device heterogeneity in federated learning cause slow convergence and poor performance in edge networks.
core_claim: FedCPSL achieves fast convergence and high performance by combining pseudo-labeling and interpolation-based personalization with adaptive variance reduction.
key_contributions: Novel FedCPSL algorithm for personalized semi-supervised FL
Theoretical guarantee of sublinear convergence under heterogeneity
Three acceleration strategies: momentum, variance reduction, normalized aggregation
novelty: First to integrate pseudo-labeling with interpolation-based personalization in FL while addressing heterogeneity through adaptive variance reduction and normalized aggregation.
method_summary: FedCPSL simultaneously optimizes global model θ, pseudo-labels νi, and local models θi,lc via alternating minimization and local SGD with three acceleration strategies.
key_innovation: Adaptive client variance reduction with control variables that correct local updates while accommodating momentum and heterogeneous local updates, enabling stable convergence.
assumptions: Partial client participation (PCP) with small fixed-size subsets
theoretical_grounding: Proves sublinear convergence rate for FedCPSL under both statistical and system heterogeneity, with resilience to client drift and objective inconsistency.
headline_result: FedCPSL achieves 98.2% accuracy on MNIST (m=0.1N) and 80.7% on CIFAR-10 (m=0.1N)
ablations: βi study: βi=0.75 optimal for personalization (Fig 1)
γ study: γ=0.8 optimal for momentum (Fig 2)
HLU robustness: FedCPSL degrades least under HLU (Fig 5, Table I)
improvement_over_baseline: FedCPSL reduces communication rounds to reach 95% accuracy by up to 60% compared to APFL/APFSL (30 rounds vs 80+). Under HLU, FedCPSL outperforms FedSHVR by 24.5% on MNIST (97.5% vs 73.0%).
best_result: FedCPSL achieves 98.2% accuracy on MNIST (m=0.1N) and 80.7% on CIFAR-10 (m=0.1N)
title: Toward Fast Personalized Semi-Supervised Federated Learning in Edge Networks: Algorithm Design and Theoretical Guarantee
